primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for foreign exchange hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Explicitly addresses the exception allowing foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements
Explains why intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated upon consolidation and represent foreign exchange exposure requiring recognition

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that hedge accounting applies only to foreign exchange differences between functional currencies of foreign operation and parent entity
Confirms that intragroup monetary items create exposure to currency fluctuations that affect consolidated profit or loss

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses measurement of dividend payables but not hedge accounting eligibility for foreign exchange risk
Classifies dividend payments in cash flow statements but does not address hedge accounting treatment of dividend receivables

treatment_families.family: Eligibility of intragroup monetary items as hedged items under IFRS 9 hedge accounting
Foreign exchange gain/loss recognition on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Foreign exchange risk of intragroup monetary item qualifies as hedged item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly permits foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items when not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary items excluded from hedge accounting in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 provides exception-based permission; without meeting the exception criteria, intragroup items may not qualify as eligible hedged items

approaches.label: Foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items recognized in profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 requires recognition of exchange differences in profit or loss unless paragraph 32 circumstances apply, supporting this as a peer approach when hedge accounting is not documented

approaches.label: Foreign exchange differences accumulated in separate component of equity
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 references recognition in other comprehensive income for circumstances described in paragraph 32 (net investment hedge), providing distinct treatment path
